mapper.select: select COALESCE(AVG(r.score), 0) from review r join lecture l on r.lect_id = l.lect_id where l.inst_id = #{instId}

select coalesce(select count(*) from payment_detail2 pd2_sub join payment p_sub on pd2_sub.payrec_id = p_sub.payrec_id where pd2_sub.lect_id = l.lect_id and p_sub.state = 1) as user_count from users u join my_cart mc on u.user_id=mc.user_id join lecture l on l.lect_id=mc.lect_id join instructor i on i.inst_id=l.inst_id where i.inst_id=#{instId}

select COALESCE( l.price * ( select count(*) from payment_detail2 pd2 join payment p2 on pd2.payrec_id = p2.payrec_id where pd2.lect_id = l.lect_id and p2.state = 1 ), 0 ) as total_revenue from lecture l join instructor i on l.inst_id = i.inst_id where i.inst_id = #{instId}

select p.time as pay_date, l.lect_id, l.name as lect_name, (l.price * ( select count(*) from payment_detail2 pd2 join payment p2 on pd2.payrec_id = p2.payrec_id where pd2.lect_id = l.lect_id and p2.state = 1 )) as total_revenue, (select count(*) from payment_detail2 pd2_sub join payment p_sub on pd2_sub.payrec_id = p_sub.payrec_id where pd2_sub.lect_id = l.lect_id and p_sub.state = 1) as user_count from payment p join payment_detail pd on p.payrec_id = pd.payrec_id join payment_detail2 pd2 on pd.payrec_id = pd2.payrec_id join lecture l on l.lect_id = pd2.lect_id where p.state = 1 and l.inst_id=#{instId} group by p.time, l.lect_id, l.name, l.price order by p.time desc
